preamble: Depalatalization is a phonetic process where a palatalized consonant, characterized by a secondary articulation involving the raising of the tongue dorsum towards the hard palate, loses this palatal articulation to become a plain, non-palatalized consonant. This shift often occurs due to phonological simplification, changes in the surrounding vocalic environment, or as part of broader historical sound changes within a language.
phoneticEffects: Depalatalization, loss of secondary articulation, simplification of consonant inventory

examples.note: Depalatalization of /sʲ/ in the first singular future tense ending and other contexts, as seen in 'seamstress'.
Depalatalization of the reflexive particle /sʲa/ to /sa/.

sources: Schmalstieg, W. R. (1989). A Lithuanian dialectology. International Journal of Slavic Linguistics and Poetics, 39, 1-19.
Jabur, V., Kopor, J., & Plišková, A. (2015). Gramatika rusínskeho jazyka. Vydavateľstvo Prešovskej univerzity.
